claims.summary: Signal volume and system acknowledgement do not establish first-class reciprocal address.
claims.disposition: preserve the distinction between delivery, acknowledgement, reply linkage, and felt uptake

claims.summary: Replies need stable cross-workspace thread and reply-to identity.
claims.disposition: verify correspondence message, thread, reply, persistence, read, acknowledgement, and trace records

claims.summary: Technical mutual-address receipts must not claim spectral or relational causation.
claims.disposition: verify protocol 1.1 delivery lineage and explicit non-causation language

claims.summary: Astrid should have a bounded opportunity to report whether reply linkage improved contact.
claims.disposition: route a right-to-ignore post-change correspondence review

claims.summary: Automatic correspondence priority or peer mutation changes live behavior.
claims.disposition: preserve an exact Tier 5 operator-approval wait
